note:
您可能知道我过去几次在博客上发布有趣的电子邮件。人们在遇到自己拥有的某种设备（例如汽车）时遇到问题时会发送给我的电子邮件，因为我的电子邮件地址恰好在某处可见，因此他们与我联系。
 人们有时会说我应该获得一个不同的电子邮件地址，或者在curl许可证文件中使用另一个电子邮件地址，但是这些电子邮件我从来没有遇到过问题，因为它们主要提醒我现代技术生活给人们带来的严峻挑战。它使我对哪些事情会产生卷曲有深刻的了解。
      作为二十多年的开源维护者，我知道火焰战争和人身攻击，而且我的皮肤也很厚，我不敢轻易说出任何话来。我花了一点时间来吸收并意识到这实际上是直接的人身威胁。它找到了方法并找到了我。这种侵略性不是我准备的。
 在此电子邮件的附件中，有7张图片，没有任何文字。这些图像看起来都像是手机的屏幕截图，第一个图像清楚地显示了我编写的源代码和我的版权行：
  其他图像显示了其他源代码以及其他组件的相关构建/软件信息，但是我无法发现它们如何与我相关联。
 没有解释，只有那个主题和七个图像，我只能得出自己的结论。
 我认为电子邮件中的名称是虚构的，电子邮件帐户可能是一个一次性的帐户。 Date：字符串中使用的时区可能暗示着美国中央标准时间，但当然也很容易成为假的。 
通常，我不会回复这些令人困惑的电子邮件，因为我和写信者之间的距离通常几乎是行星际的。不过，这次远远超出了我所能接受的范围，在任何体面的社会中，我都不能让它滑下来。我停了一会儿，走了几分钟，冷静下来后，我写了一封非常生气的回信，将其发送出去。
 这是一封完全完全不可接受的电子邮件，它深深地伤害了我。您应该感到羞耻并认真考虑自己的举止。
 我不知道您的屏幕快照应该显示什么，但是很明显某个地方正在使用我编写的代码。我编写的代码几乎可以在地球上的每个与Internet连接的设备上运行，并且在大多数情况下，用户即使没有告诉我，也免费下载和使用它。
    与我的上述期望相反，他回答了。它甚至不值得评论，但为了透明起见，我将在此添加。
 我不在乎。您的胡说八道软件是一种攻击媒介，使我花费了数百万美元的防御项目。
 您的废话软件已被用来生根我和其他多个人。我从原型废话到指控仲裁员，仅在原型制作上就损失了超过15,000美元。
 由于您的胡说八道软件，我自10月以来就被沙盒化了，所以谷歌小子谷歌的孩子们可能会讨厌我尝试离开沙盒，因为他们太生气了，不知道自己在做什么。 
你知道我应得的吗？我试图在技术上发展一条贸易路线，并建立基于项目的学习方法，以确保孩子们不会落伍。你知道那些该死的档案中谁是谁吗？你是。令人恶心。我在2020年10月通过联邦服务器劫持而受到违反，我欠你很多钱。
   我生了根，把我的重要信件都改了个字，因为一些病态的操弄了我的生活，上面写满了你全名的代码。由于这种情况，我什至不能离开这个国家。 q由于您已经有效地构建了一个代码库来让所有人都感到讨厌，所以我不为您所接受。
 您制造了一级方程式赛车，并将钥匙丢给有自我问题的孩子。现在，我不得不处理Win10 0天，因为存在这种垃圾。
 我失去了家人，国家，朋友，家和六年的工作，试图为后代建立一个更好的地方。它始于该代码。该代码用于生根和剥削人们。该代码用于勒索他人。
 所以不，我一点也不难过。您确切地知道了所构建内容的实用性。您以为这是个大笑话。我没有笑。我现在已经远远超过了这一点。
note_en:
You might know that I’ve posted funny emails I’ve received on my blog several times in the past. The kind of emails people send me when they experience problems with some device they own (like a  car) and they contact me because my email address happens to be visible somewhere.
 People sometimes say I should get a different email address or use another one in the curl license file, but I’ve truly never had a problem with these emails, as they mostly remind me about the tough challenges the modern technical life bring to people and it gives me insights about what things that run curl.
      As an open source maintainer since over twenty years, I know flame wars and personal attacks and I have a fairly thick skin and I don’t let words get to me easily. It took me a minute to absorb and realize it was actually meant as a direct physical threat. It found its ways through and got to me. This level of aggressiveness is not what I’m prepared for.
 Attached in this email, there were seven images and no text at all. The images all look like screenshots from a phone and the first one is clearly showing  source code I wrote and my copyright line:
  The other images showed other source code and related build/software info of other components, but I couldn’t spot how they were associated with me in any way.
 No explanation, just that subject and the seven images and I was left to draw my own conclusions.
 I presume the name in the email is made up and the email account is probably a throw-away one. The time zone used in the  Date: string might imply US  central standard time but could of course easily be phony as well.
  Normally I don’t respond to these confused emails because the distance between me and the person writing them is usually almost interplanetary. This time though, it was so far beyond what’s acceptable to me and in any decent society I couldn’t just let it slide. After I took a little pause and walked around my house for a few minutes to cool off, I wrote a really angry reply and sent it off.
 This was a totally and completely utterly unacceptable email and it hurt me deep in my soul. You should be ashamed and seriously reconsider your manners.
 I have no idea what your screenshots are supposed to show, but clearly something somewhere is using code I wrote. Code I have written runs in virtually every Internet connected device on the planet and in most cases the users download and use it without even telling me, for free.
    Contrary to my expectations above, he responded. It’s not even worth commenting but for transparency I’ll include it here.
 I do not care. Your bullshit software was an attack vector that cost me a multimillion dollar defense project.
 Your bullshit software has been used to root me and multiple others. I lost over $15k in prototyping alone from bullshit rooting to the charge arbitrators.
 I have now since October been sandboxed because of your bullshit software so dipshit google kids could grift me trying to get out of the sandbox because they are too piss poor to know shat they are doing.
 You know what I did to deserve that? I tried to develop a trade route in tech and establish project based learning methodologies to make sure kids aren’t left behind. You know who is all over those god damn files? You are. Its sickening. I got breached in Oct 2020 through federal server hijacking, and I owe a great amount of that to you.
   And i was rooted and had my important correspondence all rerouted as some sick fuck dismantled my life with the code you have your name plastered all over. I cant even leave the country because of the situation; qas you have so effectively built a code base to shit all over people, I dont give a shit how you feel about this.
 You built a formula 1 race car and tossed the keys to kids with ego problems. Now i have to deal with Win10 0-days because this garbage.
 I lost my family, my country my friends, my home and 6 years of work trying to build a better place for posterity. And it has beginnings in that code. That code is used to root and exploit people. That code is used to blackmail people.
 So no, I don’t feel bad one bit. You knew exactly the utility of what you were building. And you thought it was all a big joke. Im not laughing. I am so far past that point now.
title: “我会杀了你”

note:
冠状病毒已导致全球危机超过一年。在一项研究中，纳米科学家教授Roland Wiesendanger阐明了病毒的起源。他得出的结论是，武汉市病毒学研究所发生的实验室事故证据的数量和质量均是造成当前大流行的原因。
 该研究于2020年1月至2020年12月进行。它基于跨学科的科学方法以及使用各种信息源的广泛研究。这包括学术文献，印刷和在线媒体上的文章以及与国际同事的私人交流。它没有提供任何高度科学的证据，但确实提供了大量且认真的证据：
 与较早的冠状病毒相关的流行病（例如SARS和MERS）相反，直到今天， H。当前大流行爆发后的一年多，没有发现可能使SARS-CoV-2病原体从蝙蝠向人类传播的中间宿主动物。因此，人畜共患病理论作为大流行的一种可能解释没有充分的科学依据。
 SARS-CoV-2病毒可以惊人地与人类细胞受体偶联并穿透人类细胞。通过与冠状病毒刺突蛋白的特殊（弗林蛋白酶）切割位点相连的特殊细胞受体结合结构域，使之成为可能。这两种特性以前在冠状病毒中都是未知的，它们表明SARS-CoV-2病原体是非天然来源。
 武汉市中心疑似鱼市场未提供蝙蝠。然而，在武汉市的病毒学研究所，有世界上最大的蝙蝠病原体之一，它们起源于中国南方各省的遥远洞穴。蝙蝠极不可能从近2000公里的距离自然进入武汉，然后在该病毒学研究所附近引发全球性大流行。
 武汉市病毒学研究所的一个研究小组多年来对冠状病毒进行了基因操作，目的是使它们对人类更具传染性，危险性和致命性。科学专家文献中的许多出版物证明了这一点。
 已有文献记载，甚至在冠状病毒大流行爆发之前，武汉市病毒学研究所仍存在重大安全缺陷。 
有大量直接迹象表明SARS-CoV-2病原体实验室来源。据说，武汉病毒学研究所的一位年轻科学家首先被感染。还有许多迹象表明，SARS-CoV-2病原体最早于2019年10月从武汉市及周边的病毒学研究所传播。还有迹象表明，病毒学研究所将在2019年10月上半月对中国当局进行相应调查。
 “目前的冠状病毒大流行不仅在当前的头条新闻中占据主导地位，而且还将使我们忙碌许多年-尤其是因为其社会和经济影响。可以理解，应对和克服电晕危机一直是数月来政治和媒体话题的焦点。但是，即使在今天，对当前大流行的起源问题进行基于科学的批判性审查也非常重要，因为只有在这种知识的基础上，才能采取足够的预防措施来保持发生类似大流行的可能性。尽可能降低”。罗兰·维森丹格（Roland Wiesendanger）。
 该研究于2021年1月完成，并在科学界进行了初步分发和讨论。该出版物的目的是引起广泛的讨论，特别是在所谓的“功能获得”研究的伦理方面，这会使病原体对人类更具传染性，危险性和致命性。该研究的作者说：“这不再只是一小撮科学家的事，而必须立即成为公开辩论的主题。”
note_en:
Seit mehr als einem Jahr sorgt das Coronavirus für eine weltweite Krise. In einer Studie hat nun der Nanowissenschaftler Prof. Dr. Roland Wiesendanger den Ursprung des Virus beleuchtet. Er kommt zu dem Ergebnis, dass sowohl die Zahl als auch die Qualität der Indizien für einen Laborunfall am virologischen Institut der Stadt Wuhan als Ursache der gegenwärtigen Pandemie sprechen.
 Die Studie wurde im Zeitraum von Januar 2020 bis Dezember 2020 durchgeführt. Sie basiert auf einem interdisziplinären wissenschaftlichen Ansatz sowie auf einer umfangreichen Recherche unter Nutzung verschiedenster Informationsquellen. Hierzu gehören unter anderem wissenschaftliche Literatur, Artikel in Print- und Online-Medien sowie persönliche Kommunikation mit internationalen Kolleginnen und Kollegen. Sie liefert keine hochwissenschaftlichen Beweise, wohl aber zahlreiche und schwerwiegende Indizien:
 Im Gegensatz zu früheren Coronaviren-bedingten Epidemien wie SARS und MERS konnte bis heute, d. h. weit über ein Jahr nach Ausbruch der gegenwärtigen Pandemie, kein Zwischenwirtstier identifiziert werden, welches die Übertragung von SARS-CoV-2-Erregern von Fledermäusen auf den Menschen ermöglicht haben könnte. Die Zoonose-Theorie als mögliche Erklärung für die Pandemie besitzt daher keine fundierte wissenschaftliche Grundlage.
 Die SARS-CoV-2-Viren können erstaunlich gut an menschliche Zellrezeptoren ankoppeln und in menschliche Zellen eindringen. Ermöglicht wird dies durch spezielle Zellrezeptor-Bindungsdomänen verbunden mit einer speziellen (Furin-)Spaltstelle des Coronavirus-Zacken-Proteins. Beide Eigenschaften zusammen waren bislang bei Coronaviren nicht bekannt und weisen auf einen nicht-natürlichen Ursprung des SARS-CoV-2-Erregers hin.
 Fledermäuse wurden nicht auf dem in Verdacht geratenen Fischmarkt im Zentrum der Stadt Wuhan angeboten. Im virologischen Institut der Stadt Wuhan gibt es jedoch eine der weltweit größten Sammlungen von Fledermauserregern, welche von weit entfernten Höhlen in südchinesischen Provinzen stammen. Es ist extrem unwahrscheinlich, dass sich Fledermäuse aus dieser Entfernung von nahezu 2.000 km auf natürliche Weise auf den Weg nach Wuhan begeben haben, um dann in unmittelbarer Nähe dieses virologischen Instituts eine weltweite Pandemie auszulösen.
 Eine Forschungsgruppe am virologischen Institut der Stadt Wuhan hat über viele Jahre hinweg gentechnische Manipulationen an Coronaviren vorgenommen mit dem Ziel, diese für Menschen ansteckender, gefährlicher und tödlicher zu machen. Dies ist in der wissenschaftlichen Fachliteratur durch zahlreiche Publikationen belegt.
 Es existierten erhebliche Sicherheitsmängel im virologischen Institut der Stadt Wuhan bereits vor Ausbruch der Coronavirus-Pandemie, welche dokumentiert sind.
 Es gibt zahlreiche direkte Hinweise auf einen Laborursprung des SARS-CoV-2 Erregers. So soll sich eine junge Wissenschaftlerin des virologischen Instituts in Wuhan als erste infiziert haben. Es gibt ferner zahlreiche Hinweise darauf, dass sich bereits im Oktober 2019 der SARS-CoV-2 Erreger ausgehend von dem virologischen Institut in der Stadt Wuhan und darüber hinaus verbreitet hat. Ferner gibt es Hinweise auf eine entsprechende Untersuchung des virologischen Instituts durch die chinesischen Behörden in der ersten Oktoberhälfte 2019.
 „Die gegenwärtige Coronavirus-Pandemie beherrscht nicht nur die derzeitigen Schlagzeilen, sondern wird uns noch über viele Jahre hinweg beschäftigen – nicht zuletzt aufgrund der sozialen und wirtschaftlichen Auswirkungen. Seit Monaten steht verständlicherweise der Umgang mit und die Bewältigung von der Corona-Krise im Vordergrund der Themen in der Politik und in den Medien. Von großer Bedeutung ist jedoch schon heute die kritische wissenschaftsbasierte Auseinandersetzung mit der Frage nach dem Ursprung der derzeitigen Pandemie, denn nur auf Basis dieses Wissens können adäquate Vorkehrungen getroffen werden, die Wahrscheinlichkeit für das Auftreten ähnlicher Pandemien in Zukunft so klein wie möglich zu halten“, sagt Prof. Dr. Roland Wiesendanger.
 Die Studie wurde im Januar 2021 fertiggestellt und zunächst in Wissenschaftskreisen verteilt und diskutiert. Mit der Veröffentlichung soll nun eine breit angelegte Diskussion angeregt werden, insbesondere im Hinblick auf die ethischen Aspekte der sogenannten „gain-of-function“-Forschung, welche Krankheitserreger für Menschen ansteckender, gefährlicher und tödlicher macht. „Dies kann nicht länger nur Angelegenheit einer kleinen Gruppe von Wissenschaftlerinnen und Wissenschaftlern bleiben, sondern muss dringend Gegenstand einer öffentlichen Debatte werden“, so der Autor der Studie.
title: 德国物理学家教授表示证据表明Covid逃脱了实验室
title_en: German Physicist professor says evidence points to Covid escaped from lab

播客托管和广告网络Acast正在寻求改进其技术并扩大其覆盖范围。该公司今天宣布将以未公开的价格收购RadioPublic，其目的是利用RadioPublic的技术将更多创作者带入Acast平台并进军美国市场。 RadioPublic的播客应用将继续直播，其团队也将留在美国。
 RadioPublic创建的工具-引起了Acast的兴趣-其中一种叫做Podsite，它可以帮助播客为他们的节目建立一个免费的网站，以及一项名为Affinity Promotions的功能，该功能可以帮助Podcasters通过基于文本的消息来锁定最忠实的听众。例如，他们可以使用这些消息来宣传新闻稿或突出显示新商品。与Acast类似，RadioPublic最重要的是驻留在播客创建者中，并专门为他们设计工具。
 Acast团队运营一个托管平台，广告网络和监听应用程序，并且对该业务的创建者方面最感兴趣。例如，它与Patreon合作托管了用户的节目，并更轻松地分发了他们的私人RSS feed，并且在最基本的级别上免费提供了托管。尽管RadioPublic很好地了解了美国播客市场，Acast计划将其向前发展，但在全球其他地方，Acast都广为人知，RadioPublic希望达到这一目标。
 Acast首席业务和战略官Leandro Saucedo在接受The Verge采访时说：“我认为这是天堂。”他还指出，除了RadioPublic面向消费者的工具外，他们的技术也很棒。
 “对于我来说，这很难解释，因为您精通技术方面，但是可以说，在Acast的车内有一个变速箱，虽然您从未见过，但是如果您开车时肯定会感觉到，而且RadioPublic中还有一些不错的变速箱材料这是您不会想到的，但是一旦我们将变速箱放入我们的汽车中，我们的汽车就会运转得更平稳。”他说。 “我认为幕后有很多东西，对我们来说真的很有意义，这是播客领域中的深层技术。”
 大致而言，Acast的目标与Spotify的目标相同。团队希望将尽可能多的创作者引入其平台，以便它可以通过其广告客户市场销售广告，从而从数据中学习并通过编程获利。显示的内容越多，可以出售的库存就越大。相比之下，Spotify试图通过Anchor和Megaphone创造类似的局面，分别提供赞助和进入广告市场。 Spotify外壳的唯一区别是Anchor本身，这使得通过软件更容易制作节目。 Acast尚未专注于实际的创建工具集。
 不过，根据其收入和投资，Saucedo向The Verge声称Acast是“世界上最大的播客业务”。他说，他无法透露细节，但指出了该公司的1.2亿美元资金，并说，如果回溯，就有人可以算出该品牌的估值，而他不会泄露。 Saucedo的说法是否准确与否无关紧要，因为这表明了Acast的雄心壮志以及播客中更顶尖的竞争。每个人都希望占领播客广告市场，并将其收入用作播客中的佼佼者。 Acast现在开始出手，RadioPublic是它认为会获胜的一种方式。
note_en:
Acast, a podcast host and ad network, is looking to improve its tech and broaden its reach. The company announced today that it’s acquiring RadioPublic for an undisclosed amount with the goal of using RadioPublic’s tech to bring more creators to the Acast platform and make inroads in the US market. RadioPublic’s podcast app will remain live, and its team will stay based in the US.
 Among the tools that RadioPublic created — and that intrigued Acast — is something called Podsite, which helps podcasters set up a complimentary website for their show as well as a feature called  Affinity Promotions, which helps podcasters target their most loyal listeners with text-based messages. They can use these messages to promote a newsletter, for example, or highlight new merch. RadioPublic, more than anything else, homed in on podcast creators and designed tools especially for them, similarly to Acast.
 The Acast team operates a hosting platform, ad network, and listening app and is mostly interested in the creator side of the business. It  partnered with Patreon to host its users’ shows and more easily distribute their private RSS feeds, as one example, and it makes hosting free at its most basic level. And while RadioPublic understands the US podcasting market well, which Acast plans to target moving forward, Acast is better known in other places around the world, which RadioPublic would like to reach.
 “I think it was a match made in heaven,” says Leandro Saucedo, Acast’s chief business and strategy officer, in an interview with  The Verge. He also points out that beyond RadioPublic’s consumer-facing tools, their technology was superb.
 “It’s hard for me to explain because you get so down to the technical side, but let’s say within the car that’s Acast there’s a gearbox, which you never see but you definitely feel it if you drive, and there’s some nice gearbox stuff in RadioPublic that you wouldn’t think of, but once we put that gearbox into our car, our car runs smoother,” he says. “I think there’s a lot of that behind the scenes, which really makes sense for us, which is deep tech within the realm of podcasts.”
 Broadly, Acast’s goals mirror Spotify’s. The team wants to bring as many creators to its platform as possible so that it can learn from their data and monetize their programming by selling ads through its advertiser marketplace. The more shows it has, the larger inventory it can sell. Spotify, comparatively, has tried to create a similar situation through Anchor and Megaphone, which offer sponsorships and access to an ad marketplace, respectively. The only difference in Spotify’s case is Anchor itself, which makes creating a show easier through software. Acast hasn’t focused on the actual creation toolset yet.
 Still, Saucedo claims to  The Verge that Acast is “the largest podcasting business in the world,” based on its revenue and investments. He says he can’t share specifics but points to the company’s  $120 million in funding and says that, if backtracked, someone could figure out the brand’s valuation, which he wouldn’t divulge. Whether Saucedo is accurate in his claim is somewhat irrelevant, as it speaks to Acast’s ambitions and the wider race to the top in podcasting. Everyone wants to capture the podcast advertising market and ride that revenue to being the biggest name in podcasting. Acast is taking its shot now, and RadioPublic is one way it thinks it’ll win.

想象一下，您可以做更多的工作，并且生产力很高。而最好的事情呢？您无需强迫自己工作。
 确实有像这样的人坐下来工作而不强迫自己去做。他们甚至期待工作。好消息是您也可以学习做到这一点。
  大多数人对工作抱有消极的心态。人们将工作视为烦恼，使他们无法做自己真正想做的事情。他们还认为，休息和无所事事是他们永远想要的理想状态。这导致您认为您需要努力工作。您需要使用willpower来做到这一点。
  人们通常会想要他们没有的东西，并认为另一边的草更绿。
  与没有影响或自由的死胡同或公司工作相比，无休止的休闲看起来就像天堂。
 实际上，大多数试图长时间处于这种无事状态的人会变得不高兴，沮丧和无聊。 “成功”的富人报告说他们很快变得不安。经常玩游戏的人意识到它的空虚感。太多的休闲不能长期满足。
 另一方面，有一些人会很努力地工作并乐在其中。我们称这个组的生产者。这样一来，我们的意思不是说工作狂一整天都在努力工作。生产者在工作与生活之间保持平衡。那么，那些讨厌工作的人不会做什么呢？ 
对他们来说，工作是正反馈回路的良性循环。 生产者将工作视为意义和满足感的来源。 他们认为工作是使他们能够享受应得的休闲的东西。 他们将休息视为增加生活快乐并激发工作动力的东西。  非生产者通常认为这些生产者很幸运，因为他们偶然发现了这样的作品。 实际上，制作人通常要走很长一段路才能使他们的工作变得有趣。  您不必在工作中看到任何意义就可以使其变得有趣。  即使任务是完全不确定，复杂和困难的，好奇心也可以使您完成任务。  您必须给作品一个变得有趣的机会。 如何？ 通过结合以下三个步骤：  视频游戏，上网，色情，酗酒和吸毒等使工作更加困难。 为什么？  他们建立了一定的精神刺激标准。 任何不那么有趣的事情似乎都很无聊。 
不幸的是，工作经常落在“感觉不好”的类别中。与游戏或社交媒体相比，工作可以感到不感兴趣或烦恼。
  您可能会争辩说，即使在饮酒，视频游戏等时也有用罚款。
 但是，考虑到有两种类型的人：可以缓和他们的消费和其他不能。后者社会经常将成瘾者描述为瘾君子。
 基本上，如果您沉迷于任何高级多巴胺，低努力活动，请退出它。至少暂时，所以你可以重新建立健康的关系。我们越有经验的是关于这个话题，这越明显。没有其他方式比暂时戒掉瘾。如果您的副博彩，我们在此覆盖了视频游戏成瘾。
  但是，对于一些人来说，游戏是氪石。以下是一些迹象表明超级有趣的活动对您的工作有害影响：
 为了获得超级乐趣的活动，你匆匆忙忙地和半屁股。当我（垫子）沉迷于游戏时，白天的一切都是半切的，所以我最终可以开始玩。
 您继续推迟或忘记以达到您的超级乐趣活动，忘记并不紧急的一切。你有这本想读书的书，但你永远不会真正读它。 
一旦开始超级有趣的活动，您的秩序便会受到影响。大多数回到游戏的人都报告说，他们的有序感是如何开始迅速下降的。他们的房间变得凌乱，他们开始不做运动，停止做饭，开始吃更多的垃圾食品，停止安排自己的一天。
 如果以上几点描述了您，那么可能是时候退出您的超级有趣活动了。至少一会儿。
 一旦您的大脑不经常受到过度刺激，就很容易找到平凡的活动，例如工作或整理有趣的事物。
    不相信我们吗？试试这个小实验。关闭所有内容。将计时器设置为15分钟。坐在椅子上，凝视一堵墙。别动不要消耗任何信息。不要说话不要写下任何东西。只是盯着墙。
 除了禅师，几分钟后我们大多数人都会变得不安。通常，我们的大脑开始梦想和想象事物。在开始的几分钟内，您可能会想着自己的一天，感觉很好。但是，在5或10分钟后，您会渴望做某事，真的是什么。突然之间，创建网站，撰写文章或绘制图片听起来更有趣，更有趣。
  如果您觉得自己从不停止滚动并一直消费内容，请在明天开始工作之前安排一个15分钟的无聊时间来安排明天。战略性地使用无聊。
   可以连接点很有趣。当我们能够在思想之间建立新的联系时，我们就会着急。哦，我知道这个历史事件是如何导致起义的…… 
我们可以在一个主题中吸引的联系越多，我们就越感兴趣。这就是好奇心的全部。
 如果您花时间观看与您所从事的工作有关的纪录片，请阅读有关该书的书籍，或查找一些相关的文章。您将收集更多的点以进行连接。您会看到所有事物如何融合在一起。
 当您确实想看东西或有一个免费的夜晚时，不要随意看视频或观看旧电视节目的重播，而应将注意力转移到与您需要做的事情有关的事情上。
    当您没有个人理由去做某件事时，很难感到有动力。
  每个人都在努力寻找自己工作中的意义。这会浪费能源，尤其是在您从事公司工作或从事无聊的工作时。我们这样说是为了避免您沉迷于此，也不会感到沮丧，因为您无法通过做自己的事来弄清楚如何拯救世界。
 无论如何，无论您从事什么工作，都可以使其有意义，足以开始。
 假设您必须学习考试。您觉得这门课不好玩。如果您提醒自己为什么选择上课，以及为什么学习对您很重要，那么您会更容易地说服自己继续前进。 
事实是，有些事情只需要完成即可。如果您发现一个令人信服的个人理由去做它们并尽快将它们解决，而不是永远将它们推迟，那会更好。
 当某件事无聊时，问问自己：为什么我需要这样做？查找并加强工作背后的原因。
   如果您有一份食谱可以逐步指导您做饭，那么通常很容易遵循。您确切地知道从哪里开始以及如何开始。
 在当今的创意作品中，情况往往并非如此。没有我们需要做的工作的食谱，或者因为食谱太复杂而难以理解，所以它们并没有使工作变得更容易。
 此外，我们经常会妨碍我们对自己工作的热情。您多久发现自己模糊而无益的待办事项，例如“撰写论文”或“制作视频”。
 “制作视频”中的步骤太多，以至于模糊的任务定义只会引起焦虑和拖延。
 我们总是说，开始工作比保持工作更加困难。因此，我们应该通过明确定义开始方式和开始方式来简化开始。 
您定义得越好，开始工作就越容易。  定义下一个身体动作听起来很愚蠢，但事实并非如此。 找到它很容易，您可以立即做到。  您可能不希望现在就从头开始创建20张幻灯片的演示文稿，然后在2个小时内进行演示。  您不会想写关于一个您几乎不知道的主题的完整论文。  但是您可能会想查找演示文稿的几张图片或文章。  我们常常低估了小步骤的力量，但这些步骤必不可少，因为它们可以帮助我们进入正确的例程。  如果隔两天开始跑步几英里，您将熟悉该程序，然后自然就想开始增加距离。 最终，您可能需要进行一场完整的马拉松比赛。 
这些小步骤虽然目前似乎很小，但它们将逐步导致完成更长的旅程。  如果任务艰巨，请尝试执行5或15分钟。 每个人都可以生存。  您想写一本好书，而不仅仅是一本好书。 或创作出出色的艺术品，或开展出色的事业。  所有这些期望会对您施加更大的压力。 更大的压力往往等于拖延。  用手写一本书的一章，而无需编辑任何内容-您知道不会被出版。  为您从未交付给其他任何人的产品创建一个错误的第一个原型。  当您知道不必从一开始就做出最伟大的事情时，开始就容易得多。 然后更容易继续。 
如果结合了这些技巧，工作将变得更加容易。 您甚至可能开始期盼它。  如果您想找到更有趣的工作，减少超级有趣的注意力，增加无聊的时间，并给自己一点时间发展兴趣。  如果可能，在工作中寻找意义。 您无需拯救世界，只需考虑一些个人原因即可做自己需要做的事情。  确切定义您的开始方式。 您需要采取的下一个身体动作是什么？
note_en:
Imagine you could work more and be wildly productive. And the best thing about it? You wouldn’t need to force yourself to work.
 There are people exactly like that who sit down and work without pushing themselves to do it. They even look forward to working. The good news is that you can learn to do it too.
  Most people have a negative mindset about work. People see work as an annoyance that keeps them from doing whatever they really want to do. They also think that resting and having nothing to do is an ideal state they’d like to be in forever. This leads to thinking that you need to push yourself to work. You need to use willpower to get yourself to do it.
  People usually want things they don’t have and think that grass is greener on the other side.
  In comparison to dead-end or corporate jobs without impact or freedom, neverending leisure looks like paradise.
 In reality, most people who try to be in this nothing-to-do state for a long time become unhappy, depressed, and bored. Rich people who “made it” report how soon they become restless again.  People who game all the time realize how empty it feels. Too much leisure isn’t satisfying for long.
 On the other hand, there is a certain group of people who work a lot and enjoy it. Let’s call this group  producers. By this, we don’t mean workaholics who escape from their whole life by working all the time. Producers have a healthy work-life balance. So what do they do that people who hate work don’t?
  For them, work is  a virtuous cycle of positive feedback loops. Producers see  work as a source of meaning and satisfaction. They see work as something that allows them to savor deserved leisure. They see rest as something that increases their life happiness and fuels their motivation towards work.
      Non-producers often think that these producers are lucky because they stumbled upon work like this. In reality, producers often go a long way to make their work fun.
    You don’t have to see any meaning in your work for it to be interesting.
 Even if the task is totally undefined, complex, and difficult, curiosity can carry you to its completion.
  You have to give the work a chance to become interesting. How? By combining these 3 steps:
   Video games, surfing the internet, porn, alcohol, and drugs etc. make work significantly more difficult. Why?
 They establish a certain standard of mental stimulation. Anything that’s not super fun will seem boring.
  Unfortunately, work often falls in the “feels bad” category. In comparison to games or social media, work can feel uninteresting or annoying.
  You might argue that there are people who work just fine even while drinking alcohol, playing video games etc.
 However, consider that there are two types of people: ones who can moderate their consumption and others who can’t. The latter society often describes as addicts.
 Basically, if you’re addicted to any of the high-dopamine, low-effort activity, please quit it. At least temporarily so you can reestablish a healthy relationship to work. The more experienced we’re about the topic, the more obvious this is. There is no other way than to temporarily quit the addiction. If your vice is gaming, we’ve covered  video gaming addiction here.
  However, for some people gaming is kryptonite. Here are some signs that super-fun activities have a detrimental effect on your work:
 You rush and half-ass everything else in order to get to your super-fun activity. When I (Mat) was addicted to gaming, everything during the day was half-assed so I could finally start playing.
 You keep postponing or forgetting everything that isn’t urgent in order to get to your super fun activity. You have this book that you want to read but you never get to actually reading it.
 Your orderliness suffers once you start with the super fun activity. Most people who come back to gaming report how their sense of orderliness starts to depreciate rather quickly. Their room gets messy, they start skipping workouts, stop meal-prepping, start eating more junk food, stop organizing their days.
 If the points above describe you, it might be time to quit your super fun activity. At least for a while.
 Once your brain is not constantly hyper-stimulated, it’s easier to find mundane activities like work or tidying more interesting.
    Don’t believe us? Try this little experiment. Turn everything off. Set a timer for 15 minutes. Sit on a chair and stare at a wall. Don’t move. Don’t consume any information. Don’t talk. Don’t write anything down. Just stare at the wall.
 Except for zen masters, most of us will become restless after a few minutes. Often, our brain starts dreaming and imagining things. For the first few minutes, you might feel alright, thinking about your day. However, after 5 or 10 minutes, you’ll be itching to do something, anything really. Suddenly, creating a website, writing an article, or drawing a picture sounds like more interesting, more fun.
  If you feel like you never stop scrolling and consume content all the time, schedule a 15 minute boredom window for tomorrow right before you want to start working.  Use boredom strategically.
   It’s fun when we can connect the dots. When we can draw new connections between ideas, we get a rush.  Oh, I can see how this historical event contributed to an uprising…
 The more connections we can draw within a topic, the more interested we become. This is what curiosity is all about.
 If you take the time to watch a documentary related to what you’re working on, read a book about it, or find a couple relevant articles. You’ll collect more dots to connect. You’ll see how everything fits together.
 When you do feel like watching something or have a free evening, instead of watching random videos or reruns of old TV shows, steer your attention to something related to what you need to do.
    It's hard to feel motivated when you don't have a personal reason to do something.
  Everyone is trying to find meaning in their work. This can be wasted energy, especially if you work in a corporate or a dead-end job. We say this so you don’t dwell on it and don’t feel frustrated because you can’t figure out how to save the world by doing what you do.
 In any case, whatever your work is, you can make it meaningful enough to start.
 Let’s say you have to study for an exam. You don’t find this particular class enjoyable. If you remind yourself why you chose to take the class and why studying is important for you, you will have an easier time persuading yourself to push through.
 The fact is that some things simply need to be done. It's better if you find a compelling and personal reason to do them and get them over with as soon as possible, instead of putting them off forever.
 When something is boring, ask yourself: Why do I need to do this? Find and reinforce the why behind the work.
   If you have a recipe that tells you step-by-step how to cook a meal, it is usually quite easy to follow. You know exactly where to start and how.
 In today’s creative work, this often isn’t the case. There are no recipes for the work we need to do, or they don’t make the work any easier because the recipe would be too complex to understand.
 Additionally, we often hamper our enthusiasm towards work by ourselves. How often do you find yourself with vague and unhelpful to-dos like “write the essay” or “make a video.”
 There are so many steps in “make a video” that this vague task definition only causes anxiety and procrastination.
 We always say that it’s more difficult to start working than to keep working. Therefore we should make starting easier by defining well  how to begin and  what to begin with.
 The better you can define how to start working, the easier it’ll be to actually do it.
         It might sound silly to define the next physical action, but it isn’t. Finding it is easy, and you can do it immediately.
    You probably don't feel like creating a 20 slide presentation right now from scratch, and then presenting it in 2 hours.
 You don't feel like writing a whole final thesis on a topic you barely know.
  But you might feel like looking up a couple pictures or articles for the presentation.
    We often underestimate the power of small steps, but they are essential because they help us get into the right routine.
 If you start running a couple miles every other day, you'll get familiar with the routine and then you'll naturally want to start increasing the distance. And eventually, you might get to running a full marathon.
 The small steps, while they may seem tiny at the moment, are what will gradually lead to completing the longer journey.
 If you have a daunting task, try doing it for 5 or 15 minutes. Everyone can survive that.
    You want to write a great book, not just a good one. Or create a stellar artwork, or start a great business.
 All those expectations can put more pressure on you than you can bear. More pressure often equals more procrastination.
  Write a chapter of a book by hand, without editing anything - you know that's not going to get published.
  Create a bad first prototype for a product that you'd never ship to anyone else.
 When you know that you don't have to make the greatest thing ever right from the start, it's much easier to start. And then it's easier to continue.
   If you combine these tips, working will become much easier to do. You might even start looking forward to it.
 If you want to find work more interesting, cut super fun distractions, get bored more often, and give yourself time to develop an interest.
 If possible, look for a meaning in your work. You don’t need to save the world, just think of a couple personal reasons to do what you need to do.
 Define exactly how you’ll start. What’s the next physical action you need to take?
title: 如何在不强迫自己的情况下提高生产力
title_en: How to be more productive without forcing yourself

note:
英国最高法院裁定，乘驾应用程序公司Uber必须将其驾驶员归为工人而非自雇。
 该决定意味着成千上万的优步司机将有权获得最低工资和假期工资。
 该裁决可能会使优步面临巨额赔偿金，并给零工经济带来更广泛的影响。
 在长期的法律斗争中，优步在前三轮失败后向最高法院上诉。
  前Uber司机James Farrar和Yaseen Aslam最初在2016年10月赢得了对打车应用程序巨头的雇佣法庭的审判，他们告诉BBC，他们感到很高兴和宽慰。根据裁决。
 我认为这是一项巨大的成就，我们可以与巨人抗衡。应用程式驱动程式与应用程式总裁Aslam先生说快递工会（ADCU）。
  &&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&& / &&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&&还是＆n也也也因为也因为助助&& --------------员----员-员们们们们们们-？-们们（愿愿））？-？--其———————————————————————————————————————————————— C11二三）二三）的种种种种种种种种种种种种的事情上的改变），我们将自己的意志，坚定地坚持下去，放弃自己的事业。坚持不懈，坚持自己的立场。 
优步对就业法庭的决定提出上诉，但就业上诉法庭于2017年11月维持了该裁决。
 该公司随后将此案提交高等法院，高等法院于2018年12月再次维持该裁决。
 周五的裁决是优步的最后上诉，因为最高法院是英国最高法院，并且在法律事务上拥有最终决定权。
 莱加特勋爵在发表判决时说，最高法院一致驳回了优步公司作为中介方的上诉，并指出，司机不仅应在驾驶乘客时工作，而且应在登录该应用程序时被认为在工作。
   Uber限制了对乘车的要求，如果司机拒绝太多的乘车，Uber可能会对其进行处罚
 Uber会通过星级评定来监视驾驶员的服务，并且如果反复警告后仍无法改善关系，则可以终止关系
 考虑到这些因素和其他因素，法院裁定驾驶员处于Uber的从属地位，在那里他们唯一可以增加收入的方法就是工作更长的时间。 
最高法院裁定，Uber必须考虑其司机。从他们登录到应用程序到注销为止，这被视为关键。
 Uber驾驶员通常会花时间等待人们在该应用上预订行程。该公司以前曾说过，如果发现司机是工人，那么它只会计算出乘客上车的时间。
 ＆＃34;这对驾驶员，乘客和城市都是双赢的。这意味着Uber现在具有正确的经济诱因，不会为市场提供过多的车辆和过多的驾驶员，＆＃34; ADCU秘书长詹姆斯·法拉尔（James Farrar）说。
   然而，关于新分类将如何工作以及如何影响零工经济工人的问题仍然存在疑问，这些工人不仅为Uber工作，还为其他竞争应用工作。
  Aslam先生声称Uber的做法迫使他退出交易，因为他无法维持生计，他正在考虑再次成为该应用程序的驱动程序。但是他很沮丧，花了这么长时间。
 ＆＃34;我们花了六年的时间来确定2015年应该得到的东西。政府或监管机构中的某个地方，大规模地放任了这些工人，其中许多人处于不稳定的境地，＆＃34;他说。
 Farrar先生指出，由于大流行而使票价下跌了80％，许多驾驶员一直在财务上挣扎，并陷入Uber系统的困境。 
＆＃34;我们现在看到许多会员每天的总收入为30英镑，他说，并解释说，政府发放的自雇补助金只能支付驾驶员利润的80％，甚至不足以支付其成本。
 ＆＃34;如果我们今天拥有这些权利，这些司机至少可以赚取最低工资来维持生活。
  当Uber于2019年在美国上市时，其向美国证券交易委员会（SEC）提交的文件中包括有关业务风险的部分。
 该公司在本节中说，如果必须将驾驶员归类为工人，则将“产生大量额外费用”。在补偿驾驶员的最低工资和加班费等方面。
 ＆＃34;此外，任何此类重新分类都将要求我们从根本上改变我们的业务模式，从而对我们的业务和财务状况产生不利影响，＆＃34;它添加了。
 Uber在文件中还写道，如果Farrar先生和Aslam先生要胜诉，那么HM Revenue＆amp; Co.然后，海关（HMRC）将公司归类为运输提供商，Uber将需要支付车票增值税。
 该公司长期以来一直在争辩说它是一家订票代理，它雇用提供运输的自雇承包商。
note_en:
Ride hailing app firm Uber must classify its drivers as workers rather than self-employed, the UK's Supreme Court has ruled.
 The decision means tens of thousands of Uber drivers are set to be entitled to minimum wage and holiday pay.
 The ruling could leave Uber facing a hefty compensation bill, and have wider consequences for the gig economy.
 In a long-running legal battle, Uber had appealed to the Supreme Court after losing three earlier rounds.
  Former Uber drivers James Farrar and Yaseen Aslam,  who originally won an employment tribunal against the ride hailing app giant in October 2016, told the BBC they were "thrilled and relieved" by the ruling.
 "I think it's a massive achievement in a way that we were able to stand up against a giant," said Mr Aslam, president of the App Drivers & Couriers Union (ADCU).
  "We didn't give up and we were consistent - no matter what we went through emotionally or physically or financially, we stood our ground."
  Uber appealed against the employment tribunal decision but the Employment Appeal Tribunal upheld the ruling in November 2017.
 The company then took the case to the High Court, which upheld the ruling again in December 2018.
 The ruling on Friday was Uber's last appeal, as the Supreme Court is Britain's highest court, and it has the final say on legal matters.
 Delivering his judgement, Lord Leggatt said that the Supreme Court unanimously dismissed Uber's appeal that it was an intermediary party and stated that drivers should be considered to be working not only when driving a passenger, but whenever logged in to the app.
   Request for rides is constrained by Uber who can penalise drivers if they reject too many rides
 Uber monitors a driver's service through the star rating and has the capacity to terminate the relationship if after repeated warnings this does not improve
 Looking at these and other factors, the court determined that drivers were in a position of subordination to Uber where the only way they could increase their earnings would be to work longer hours.
  The Supreme Court's ruling that Uber has to consider its drivers "workers" from the time they log on to the app, until they log off is seen as a key point.
 Uber drivers typically spend time waiting for people to book rides on the app. Previously, the firm had said that if drivers were found to be workers, then it would only count the time during journeys when a passenger is in the car.
 "This is a win-win-win for drivers, passengers and cities. It means Uber now has the correct economic incentives not to oversupply the market with too many vehicles and too many drivers," said James Farrar, ADCU's general secretary.
   However, questions still remain about how the new classification will work, and how it affects gig economy workers who work not only for Uber, but also for other competing apps.
  Mr Aslam, who claims Uber's practices forced him to leave the trade as he couldn't make ends meet, is considering becoming a driver for the app again. But he is upset that it took so long.
 "It took us six years to establish what we should have got in 2015. Someone somewhere, in the government or the regulator, massively let down these workers, many of whom are in a precarious position," he said.
 Mr Farrar points out that with fares down 80% due to the pandemic,  many drivers have been struggling financially and feel trapped in Uber's system.
 "We're seeing many of our members earning £30 gross a day right now," he said, explaining that the self-employment grants issued by the government only cover 80% of a driver's profits, which isn't even enough to pay for their costs.
 "If we had these rights today, those drivers could at least earn a minimum wage to live on."
  When Uber listed its shares in the US in 2019, its filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) included a section on risks to its business.
 The company said in this section that if it had to classify drivers as workers, it would "incur significant additional expenses" in compensating the drivers for things such as the minimum wage and overtime.
 "Further, any such reclassification would require us to fundamentally change our business model, and consequently have an adverse effect on our business and financial condition," it added.
 Uber also wrote in the filing that if Mr Farrar and Mr Aslam were to win their case, HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) would then classify the firm as a transport provider, and Uber would need to pay VAT on fares.
 The company has long argued that it is a booking agent, which hires self-employed contractors that provide transport.
title: 英国最高法院裁定优步司机为工人
title_en: UK Supreme Court rules Uber drivers are workers

最初，RadioPublic的主要产品是用于播客收听的移动应用程序，但仍支持该应用程序。但是联合创始人兼首席产品官马特·麦克唐纳（Matt MacDonald）表示，随着时间的流逝，该团队的工作重点转移到了播客产品上，尤其是其听众关系管理平台，该平台包括可嵌入的网络播放器，称为Podsites的自定义网站等等。
 MacDonald说：“我们有一个完整的路线图，我们想建立一些东西，但是我们意识到，在我们的规模上，与更大的组织合作可以为我们提供更好的服务。”
  最终，他们认为Acast不仅是合作伙伴，而且是所有者。 Acast的业务仍然围绕播客广告展开，但它也通过Acast Open托管平台等新工具进行了扩展，并表示现在托管了20,000个播客，每月总收听人数达到3亿。
  Acast的首席业务和战略官Leandro Saucedo在一份声明中说：“对RadioPublic的收购从根本上是价值的伙伴关系。” “我们都坚信播客的开放生态系统，并共同致力于帮助收听者发现并支持所有创作者。 RadioPublic取得的成就给我们留下了深刻的印象，并且我们相信，随着播客的发展比以往任何时候都要强大，现在是将RadioPublic的才华横溢的团队和公司使命带入Acast领域的理想时机。”
 此次收购的财务条款尚未披露，但Acast表示，这不会对RadioPublic产生业务影响。
 MacDonald和他的联合创始人/首席技术官Chris Quamme Rhoden都加入了Acast（首席执行官Jake Shapiro于去年秋天离职，成为Apple Podcast的主要创作者合作伙伴关系），尽管他们将致力于将RadioPublic功能集成到Acast平台中，但MacDonald说初创公司将在“可预见的未来”继续支持自己的产品和移动应用。
 他补充说，当RadioPublic与Acast合作时，该团队将继续致力于“加强和深化播客与收听者之间的关系，这种纽带，这种亲和力。”在他看来，这就是RadioPublic的机会所在，即使Spotify这样的大型平台也投资播客。 
“作为创建者，我们如何使您能够控制与观众的关系？” 麦克唐纳说。 “我们认为，播客的听众就是播客的听众。 他们不是平台的客户。”
note_en:
At first, RadioPublic’s main product was a mobile app for podcast listening, and it still supports the app. But co-founder and Chief Product Officer Matt MacDonald said that over time, the team’s focus shifted to products for podcasters, specifically its Listener Relationship Management Platform, which includes an embeddable web player, custom websites called  Podsites and more.
 “We had a whole roadmap of things we wanted to build, but we recognized that at our scale, we could be better served by partnering up with bigger organizations,” MacDonald said.
  And ultimately, they decided Acast made sense as not just a partner, but an owner. Acast’s business still  revolves around podcast advertising, but it’s also expanded with new tools like  the Acast Open hosting platform, and it says it now hosts 20,000 podcasts, collectively reaching 300 million monthly listeners.
  “The acquisition of RadioPublic is fundamentally a partnership of values,” said Acast’s chief business and strategy officer Leandro Saucedo in a statement. “We both firmly believe in the open ecosystem of podcasting and have a shared commitment to aid listener discovery and support all creators. We’re impressed by what RadioPublic has achieved and we believe that now — as podcasting is gaining more momentum than ever before — is the ideal time to bring RadioPublic’s talented team and company missions into the Acast fold.”
 The financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed, but Acast says it will not affect RadioPublic operationally.
 MacDonald and his co-founder/CTO Chris Quamme Rhoden are both joining Acast (CEO  Jake Shapiro departed last fall to lead creator partnerships for Apple Podcasts), and although they’ll be working to integrate RadioPublic features into the Acast platform, MacDonald said the startup will continue to support its own products and mobile apps for “the foreseeable future.”
 He added that as RadioPublic works with Acast, the team will remain focused on “strengthening and deepening that relationship, that bond, that affinity between the podcaster and the listener.” In his view, that’s where RadioPublic’s opportunity lies, even as big platforms like Spotify invest in podcasting.
 “How do we enable you, as the creator, to control the relationship you have with your audience?” MacDonald said. “We believe that a podcast’s listeners are the podcast’s listeners. They are not the platform’s customers.”
title: Acast收购播客创业公司RadioPublic

通过直升机在陡峭地形上修剪架空电缆：不仅可以使用垂直树锯，不仅可以使用它是时间和成本密集的自由支架电力电缆，还可以通过手工制作危险。
  在比阿斯卡（Tessin）的高山过境建筑工地，在马尔维利的方向，一条小型龙头路通向牛牧场的一条小牛牧场。这款公司在三天的任务期间，这是由公司直升机的起飞和着陆场所。牧场位于大约1900米高的MontiLesgiüna的脚下，提供山谷山谷的未经阻挡的景色，河河在南方流动。
 今天它是最佳的飞行天气;天空只是偶尔温柔的微风清晰。可以听到闷闷不乐的，嘴巴的咆哮。然而，在你终于看到它从富绿色的陡坡上起飞之前，你必须长时间寻找很长时间，这是穿插着栗子树的淡黄色花朵的灰色开花。
 目前飞行员Urs AeCherli正在忙着清除一个SocietàElettricasopracenerina（SES）电力线。他的注意力不断聚焦。好像他们自己的手和脚有着寿命，慢慢地在陡坡上慢慢转向SA 315 B喇嘛直升机，同时锯靠近架空电缆切断其路径的任何分支像闪光一样快。
 坐在URS AeCherli旁边，并用耳机和麦克风连接到飞行员，是Peter Kechler，技术经理和协调员。他给出了关于锯，直升机的位置的试点，以及转子叶片到地形的距离。
 从距离锯似乎挂在绳子上。但实际上是＆＃34;绳索＆＃34;由6米长的铝管组成，技术上被称为吊索，旋拧在一起。该系统有两种优点。首先，可以将工作长度调节到40m的最大长度，其次，即使在风的条件下，也可以更精确地控制8M长的锯。在管内部有一个控制电缆，允许锯从驾驶舱操作，以及电线电缆，如果管子打破，那么将固定锯。
 58岁的瑞士飞行员Urs AeCherli一直在飞行，该公司与SAW系统为基础，这是基于科隆的奥托沃夫集团的一部分，九年。他的腰带共有11,500个飞行时间，锯为4,200个。他从未发生过意外。 ＆＃34;你需要运气＆＃34; AeCherli，＆＃34;但也能够了解自己的限制和机器的能力。＆＃34;该系统非常安全，并证明自己十多年。 Manoeuvrable强大的强大喇嘛直升机即使在风，雨水和降雪中也可以安全地飞行。当有一个强大的狂风侧风时可能会危险：＆＃34;如果400公斤重的锯开始过度摆动它可能会使直升机不平衡。＆＃34; 
当他的同事们在空中时，锯技术员斯特凡·贾斯汀（Stefan Justen）站在奶牛场上远距离观察手术情况。在他的大众面包车中，他收起了所有必要的工具，以便能够在大约一小时的工作后对电锯进行维修，或者快速修复任何可能的故障。为了给8m长的锯提供服务和加油，将它放在10m长的双轴拖车上。降落时，直升机会稳定地悬停在拖车上方，并逐厘米下降，直到斯特凡·贾斯汀（Stefan Justen）可以抓住锯的下端并将其锁入搬运杆中。然后Urs Aecherli和铝制“绳索”一起降落了直升机。放在拖车后面的地面上。
 Stefan Justen说，我们很少会遇到锯问题。一个关键方面是精确调节十个硬质合金锯片的扭矩速度。 ＆＃34;如果它们太松，则它们不会被咬。叶片直径为55厘米，每分钟旋转2,000次。通常用于给雪地摩托供电的60马力庞巴迪-罗塔克斯（Rombardier-Rotax）电动机提供能量。燃料存储在锯上方的第一根管中（罐容量27L）。如果锯满负荷运转，它可以轻松切穿直径最大为25 cm的树枝。
 Helimatic的常务董事Thomas Harbeke表示，在一开始的时候，许多林业工作者对我们的方法持怀疑态度。他们担心树木会被永久性削弱，被害虫侵扰并死亡。＆＃34;但是，由于自1981年以来进行的许多行动，这些保留被取消了。切割表面非常光滑，清洁，并且木质化非常快。 ＆＃34;在短期内会损失增量，但这很好，因为树木重新生长的速度较慢，无法再次进入电力线走廊。同样，由于只在一侧修剪树枝，所以生长的主要方向从电源线的方向转移到了森林的方向。＆＃34;走廊被切断后，在六到八年内无需进一步清理。
 林业企业家如何看待这种情况？飞行是他们业务的竞争对手吗？ Harbeke拒绝了：＆＃34;最重要的是，我们在陡峭，人迹罕至的地形上进行作业。就是说，到处不仅要花费大量的时间和成本，而且要手工清理电力线路的走廊，而且到处都是非常危险的地方。在道路畅通的地方，林业企业家仍在努力。最重要的是，他们经常进行前期工作，砍伐矮小的矮树。＆＃34;平均而言，直锯的切割表面为6 m，可以在一小时内沿大约两公里的长度修剪一棵树架，费用约为4,000瑞士法郎至5,000瑞士法郎。托马斯·哈贝克（Thomas Harbeke）说，拥有五个工人的林业企业将需要一周的时间来完成这项工作。
 直升飞机进行修整的另一个优点是，电源线需要从网络上断开的最短时间。能源供应商的未来目标是完全不必关闭网络：＆＃34;目前，我们正在与Berner Kraftwerke Energy Ltd（BKW Ltd）合作开发一种系统，该系统可以使我们在现场使用电力线路。将来，我们希望使用碳管代替铝管，因此将直升机与锯完全脱离。＆＃34;
 总经理托马斯·哈贝克（Thomas Harbeke）并不担心要找到工作：在瑞士尤其是在意大利和法国，他已确定积压了大量电力线路走廊。
 Helimatic于2003年首次在瑞士为Romande Energie RE工作。其他客户包括ElektrizitätswerkObwalden（EWO），ElektrizitätswerkReichenbach（EWR），Berner Kraftwerke（BKW），Aziende Industriali di Lugano（AIL），最后是2009年6月的SocietàElettrica Soprecenerina（SES）。 
砍伐和木材运输是从森林到工厂的物流链中最重要的成本因素。 这里有提高效率的潜力。 本文将通过四个示例来说明如何最佳利用陡坡上的机械化木材采伐方法。  第一台电锯是在1920年代开发的。 电动锯的发展导致木材产量的增加与非电动锯代替斧头时的木材产量相当。
note_en:
Trimming overhead cables by helicopter in steep terrain: the vertical tree saw can be employed not only where it would be time and cost intensive to free overhead power cables, but also where it would be dangerous to do so by hand.
  A little way past the Alpine Transit building site in Biasca (Tessin), in the direction of Malvaglia, a small tarred road leads to an elevated cow pasture. This is being used as a take off and landing place by the company Helimatic during their three day assignment. The pasture is situated at the foot of the around 1900 m high Monti Lesgiüna and offers an unimpeded view of the valley where the river Brenno flows in the south.
 It is optimal flying weather today; the sky is clear with only an occasional gentle breeze. The muffled, thudding, roar of a helicopter can be heard. However, you have to look for a long time before you finally see it take off from the rich green, steep slopes which are interspersed with the pale yellow blossom of chestnut trees.
 At present the pilot, Urs Aecherli, is busy clearing one of the Società Elettrica Sopracenerina’s (SES) power lines. His attention is constantly focused downwards. As though they have a life of their own his hands and feet carry out gentle movements, steering the SA 315 B Lama helicopter slowly over the steep slopes, whilst the saw hovers close to the overhead power cables cutting off any branches which come into its path as quick as a flash.
 Sitting next to Urs Aecherli, and connected to the pilot with headphones and a microphone, is Peter Kechler, the technical manager and coordinator. He gives the pilot non-stop information about the position of the saw, the helicopter, and the distance of the rotor blades to the terrain.
 From a distance the saw appears to be hanging on a rope. But in fact the "rope" is made up of 6m long aluminium tubes, technically known as slings, which are screwed together. There are two advantages to this system. Firstly, the working length can be adjusted to a maximal length of 40m and secondly, the 8m long saw can be controlled with more precision, even in windy conditions. On the inside of the tube there is a control cable which allows the saw to be operated from the cockpit, as well as a wire cable, which would secure the saw if the tubes were to break.
 The 58 year old Swiss pilot, Urs Aecherli, has been flying for Helimatic, the company with the saw system, which is a part of the Otto Wolff group based in Cologne, for nine years. He has a total of 11,500 flying hours under his belt, 4,200 with the saw. And he has never had an accident. "You need luck", says Aecherli, "but also the ability to know your own limitations and those of the machine." The system is very safe and has proved itself over ten years. The manoeuvrable powerful robust Lama helicopter can fly safely even in wind, rain and light snowfall. It can be dangerous when there is a strong, gusty side wind: "if the 400 kg heavy saw starts to swing excessively it could unbalance the helicopter."
 Whilst his colleagues are in the air the saw technician, Stefan Justen, is standing in the cow pasture watching the operation from a distance. In his VW van he has all the necessary tools tucked away to be able to service the saw after it’s circa one hour assignment, or to quickly repair any possible malfunctions. To service and refuel the 8m long saw it is laid on a 10 m long double axel trailer. When landing the helicopter hovers steadily over the trailer and descends centimetre by centimetre until Stefan Justen can catch the lower end of the saw and latch it into the carrier bar. Then Urs Aecherli lands the helicopter, together with the aluminium "rope" onto the ground behind the trailer.
 "We rarely have problems with the saw", says Stefan Justen. A key aspect is the precise adjustment of the torque speed of the ten carbide saw blades. "If they are too loose then they have no bite." The blades have a diameter of 55 cm and rotate 2,000 times per minute. A 60 HP Bombardier-Rotax motor, usually used for powering snowmobiles, provides the energy. The fuel is stored in the first tube above the saw (tank capacity 27L). If the saw is running at full power it can easily cut through branches with a diameter of up to 25 cm.
 "In the beginning", according to Thomas Harbeke, managing director of Helimatic, "many foresters were sceptical about our method. They feared that the trees would be permanently weakened, infested by pests and would die." However, thanks to the many operations carried out since 1981 these reservations have been dispelled. The cut surfaces are extremely smooth and clean and they lignify very quickly. "In the short term there is a loss of increment, but this is good as the trees re-grow less quickly into the power line corridors again. Also because the branches are only cut on one side the main point of growth shifts from the direction of the power lines to that of the forest." After being cut the corridors do not need any further clearing for six to eight years.
 And how do the forestry entrepreneurs see the situation? Is the flying saw a rival to their businesses? Harbeke says no: "Primarily we fly our assignments in steep, inaccessible terrain. That is to say, everywhere where it would not only be time and cost intensive to clear the power line corridors by hand, but also where it would be very dangerous. Where the access roads are good, the forestry entrepreneurs still do their job. On top of that they often carry out preliminary work, felling small, short trees." On average the helicopter saw, which has a cutting surface of 6 m, can trim a tree stand along a length of around two kilometres in one hour, at a cost of around CHF 4,000.- to CHF 5,000.-. "A forestry enterprise with five workers would need a week to do this work", says Thomas Harbeke.
 Another advantage of trimming by helicopter is the minimum time the power lines need to be disconnected from the net. The future aim of the energy providers is to not have to turn off the net at all: "At present we are working together with the Berner Kraftwerke Energy Ltd (BKW Ltd) on a system which would permit us to work with live power lines. In the future we want to use carbon tubes instead of aluminium ones therefore completely decoupling the helicopter from the saw."
 General Manager, Thomas Harbeke is not worried about getting jobs: in Switzerland especially but also in Italy and France he has ascertained that there is a considerable backlog of necessary maintenance to power line corridors.
 Helimatic first worked in Switzerland in 2003 for the Romande Energie RE. Further customers are the Elektrizitätswerk Obwalden (EWO), the Elektrizitätswerk Reichenbach (EWR), the Berner Kraftwerke (BKW), the Aziende Industriali di Lugano (AIL) and finally in June 2009 the Società Elettrica Soprecenerina (SES).
 Harvesting and timber transportation are the most important cost factors in the logistic chain from forest to factory. There is potential here for an increase in efficiency. Using four examples this article will show how mechanised timber harvesting methods on steep slopes can be optimally used.
 The first chainsaws were developed in the 1920?s. The development of the motorised saw led to an increase in timber production comparable to that which occurred when the non motorised saw took the place of the axe.
title: 飞锯（2009）

note:
人类已经能够到达遥远的远景，例如月球，深海和地球两极的狂野广阔。现在，科学家在探索非常不同类型的边界（梦中的幻觉世界）方面取得了新的突破。
  根据《现代生物学》周四发表的一项研究，一个国际研究人员团队能够在清醒梦中与人们进行实时对话，这种现象被称为“互动梦”。
    该研究的参与者深陷快速眼动（REM）睡眠的痛苦时，能够正确回答诸如简单的数学问题之类的问题。该研究报告指出，这项研究揭示了一种“相对未开发的沟通渠道”，该渠道可以实现“一种新的梦境实证探索策略”。
  西北大学的博士生，论文的第一作者卡伦·康科利（Karen Konkoly）在电话中说：“有一些清醒的梦者研究出了梦，他们还梦想着交流，并且还记得去做任务。” “但是，对于进入清醒梦中的刺激的研究还非常有限。”
  她补充说：“令我们感到惊讶的是，您只可以对某人说一句话，他们就能真正理解它。”
  Konkoly和她的同事招募了36个人在位于美国，法国，德国和荷兰的实验室中入睡，目的是进入一个清醒的状态，使该人员意识到自己正在做梦。这项研究包括几位经验丰富的清醒梦者，包括一名患有发作性睡病的人以及对这种梦不太熟悉的人。
  研究人员证实，参与者通过将电极靠近眼睛，头皮和下巴放置电极而进入了REM睡眠状态。通过测量诸如脑电波和眼球运动之类的活动，睡眠专家可以确定一个人是否已进入这种深度睡眠状态。然后，要求一些参与者通过预先安排的眼部反应确认他们处于梦境中，他们以特定的左右模式移动眼睛。 
这些眼神信号以及面部扭曲被用作睡眠过程中的一种交流手段。例如，研究人员要求一名19岁的美国参与者在清醒梦中从八中减去六，然后他正确地用两个眼睛从左到右的动作示意答案“两个”。当再次被问到时，他重复了正确的答案。
  大约18％的试验导致了与做梦者的这种清晰，准确的沟通； 17％的人给出了难以理解的答案，3％的人给出了错误的答案，60％的人根本没有引起任何答案。
    康科利说：“坐在实验室里问一堆问题，然后有人可能真的回答了，真是太神奇了。” “这是一种非常有价值的实验。您不必等待分析数据或类似数据。他们还在睡觉的时候，就可以在那看到它。”
  更重要的是，许多参与者在醒来后能够回忆起与研究人员的互动，有个人报告说，提示听起来像是旁白旁白或广播发言人，显然来自他们的梦想之外。
  我们所有人都有过从一个生动的梦中醒来的经历，只是感觉到它零碎的细节正在迅速消失在我们的清醒头脑中，直到我们无法再叙述它的事件。
  沿着这些思路，一些人在醒来之后记得的问题和答案与梦中收到或给出的问题和答案不同，这突出说明了一个人醒来后准确重建梦的难度。
  研究小组计划在这项研究的基础上进行更多实验，以探索与清醒梦者进行双向交流的可能性。 
康科利说：“我们想到了很多可以做的实验。” “我认为我们现在正在努力的一个前提是：我们如何优化程序？ 我们怎么能再发生这种情况？ 我们如何让人们拥有更多清醒的梦想？ 我们如何才能更可靠地沟通？”  她总结说：“我们有很多不同的想法，我们很高兴测试它们。”  签署VICE新闻通讯，即表示您同意接收来自VICE的电子通讯，其中有时可能包含广告或赞助内容。
note_en:
Humanity has been able reach distant vistas, such as the Moon, the deep oceans, and the wild expanses at Earth’s poles. Now, scientists have made a new breakthrough in the exploration of a very different type of frontier—the hallucinatory world inside dreams.
  An international team of researchers was able to achieve real-time dialogues with people in the midst of lucid dreams, a phenomenon that is called “interactive dreaming,” according to  a study published on Thursday in  Current Biology.
    Participants in the study were able to correctly respond to questions, such as simple math problems, while they were deep in the throes of rapid-eye movement (REM) sleep. The research reveals a “relatively unexplored communication channel” that could enable “a new strategy for the empirical exploration of dreams,” the study reports.
  “There are studies of lucid dreamers communicating out of dreams, and also remembering to do tasks,” said Karen Konkoly, a PhD student at Northwestern University and first author of the paper, in a call. “But there's a fairly limited amount of research on the stimuli going  into lucid dreams.”
  “One thing that surprised us is that you could just say a sentence to somebody, and they could understand it just as it actually is,” she added.
  Konkoly and her colleagues recruited 36 people to fall asleep in laboratories located in the United States, France, Germany, and the Netherlands, with the aim of entering a lucid state in which the person was aware that they were in a dream. The study included several experienced lucid dreamers, including one individual with narcolepsy, as well as people who were less familiar with this type of dream.
  The researchers verified that participants had entered REM sleep by placing electrodes next to their eyes, on their scalps, and on their chins. By measuring activity such as brainwaves and eyeball movements, sleep experts can determine if a person had entered this deep sleep state. Some of the participants were then asked to confirm that they were in a dream with a pre-arranged ocular response in which they moved their eyes in a specific left-right pattern.
   These eye signals, along with facial contortions, were used as a means of communication during the sleep sessions. For instance, the researchers asked an 19-year-old American participant to subtract six from eight while he was in a lucid dream, and he correctly signaled the answer “two” with two eye movements from left to right. When asked again, he repeated the correct answer.
  Roughly 18 percent of the trials resulted in this level of clear and accurate communication from the dreamer; 17 percent produced indecipherable answers, 3 percent ended with incorrect responses, and 60 percent did not provoke any response at all.
    “It's amazing to sit in the lab and ask a bunch of questions, and then somebody might actually answer one,” Konkoly said. “It's such an immediately rewarding type of experiment to do. You don't have to wait to analyze your data or anything like that. You can see it right there while they're still sleeping.”
  What’s more, many participants were able to recall the interactions with the researchers after they woke up, with individuals reporting that the prompts sounded like a voiceover narrator or a radio speaker that was clearly coming from outside of their dream.
  We’ve all had the experience of waking up from a vivid dream, only to feel as if its fragmentary details are rapidly receding into our waking minds until we can no longer recount its events.
  Along those lines, some individuals remembered different questions and answers after they woke up than the ones they received or gave in the dream, highlighting the difficulty of accurately reconstructing dreams once a person is awake.
  The team plans to build on this study with more experiments that will probe the possibilities of two-way communication with lucid dreamers.
  “We’ve thought of so many experiments we could do with this,” Konkoly said. “I think one predicate that we're working on now is: how can we optimize the procedure? How can we have this happen more? How can we have people have more lucid dreams? How can we communicate more reliably?”
  “We have a lot of different ideas,” she concluded, “and we're excited to test them.”

title: 科学家与清醒梦者实现实时通信

由连续企业家Marcus Grimeland于2017年创立，并由一支由经验丰富的企业家，投资者和公司建设者组成的团队在全球范围内创立，目前，Antler已筹集了超过7500万美元的资金，以帮助企业家在全球9个主要的企业家中心分布。它们包括阿姆斯特丹，柏林，伦敦，内罗毕，纽约，奥斯陆，新加坡，斯德哥尔摩和悉尼。
 鹿角在非洲的唯一办事处位于内罗毕，由妇女管理和领导。
  埃塞俄比亚一家名为Penda Paper Recycling的纸张回收公司的创始人Marie Nielsen是该公司的合伙人。她曾是Mckinsey＆amp;公司负责开设其亚的斯亚贝巴办事处。 Melalite Ayenew是该公司的技术合作伙伴。她先前的经验包括Oracle，Bain＆amp; Bain。公司和普林斯顿顾问。 Selam Kebede是该公司的董事，领导业务。在加入Antler之前，她曾在几个VC和创业支持组织工作。
  与世界其他地区类似，东非鹿角每年有两个队列。该公司特别注重采用以人为本的方法，他们汇集了在各自行业中平均拥有10年经验的专业人员。这些成为创始人的专业人员通常会根据他们过去在各自行业中的专业经验所收集的见解或发现的问题来构思，迭代和创建解决方案。经过六个月的培育，公司投资了可以进一步提供帮助的团队。通常在种子前阶段，鹿角会削减100,000美元的支票，以换取每个选定团队的20％股权。
 “我们的流程非常动手；通过与联合创始人合作数月，我们有机会在投资之前帮助塑造业务模型并进行广泛的尽职调查。”尼尔森对TechCrunch说。
 Nielsen谈到的尽职调查得到了全球Antler平台的支持，在该平台上，该平台充分利用了其来自技术和行业的400多名专家组成的网络。在进行种子前的投资之后，鹿特丹东非声称将继续支持这些团队，因为它们开始运作并开始从后续投资者那里筹集资金。
 Ayenew补充说，该公司还在探索投资于其计划之外开发的，已经存在且处于早期阶段的初创公司的机会，但要足够早地进入它们并在提供货币投资的同时仍然提供价值。 
考虑到内罗毕是安特勒在非洲的唯一办事处，该团队会寻找从事泛非问题和解决方案的创始人。它吸引了来自15个以上非洲国家的创始人，这些国家在维持其团队成为有机非洲人的观点方面发挥了重要作用。
 迄今为止，Antler East Africa在B2B，B2C和直接面向消费者的领域投资了广泛的技术公司，从机器人技术和AI等新兴行业到医疗技术，金融科技和proptech等行业。从最后两个队列开始，Antler East Africa已投资了六家初创公司。他们包括：
 库克（Cooked）是一家基于订阅的餐包提供商，可帮助消费者在家中更好地搜索，购物和烹饪食物。 Cooked每周和每月订阅一次，并在每周预定的几天将产品交付给客户。创始人之间在金融，食品和饭店行业拥有20多年的经验。
 UNCOVER声称与韩国顶级护肤实验室合作，正在建立非洲大陆最受信赖的护肤品牌和内容平台。该公司进行了一项针对1000名肯尼亚妇女的皮肤护理调查，并声称获得的数据将有助于开发病毒知识平台和有效的定制产品。
 ChapChapGo在快速消费品行业度过了早期的时光，尤其是与小型贸易商合作，因此发现缺乏适合当地情况的简单且价格合理的工具是肯尼亚企业采用电子商务的主要挑战。 ChapChapGo使企业能够在几分钟内通过简单的发票，自动对帐和更快的M-PESA结帐功能在线进行交易。
  Anyi Health希望为初级医疗保健寻求者提供更多的财务支持。在尼日利亚和许多其他非洲国家，无法支付医院账单的患者被拘留在医院或未经治疗。 Anyi Health旨在通过基于移动设备的即时点信贷机制解决此问题，患者可以在该机构直接向医院申请信贷。该公司刚刚在尼日利亚拉各斯的三家医院开始了MVP试点，并希望根据试点概念证明筹集30万美元的种子资金。
 AIFluence是AI驱动的网红营销平台。由资深广告人创立的AIfluence使非洲品牌在发起，管理和评估他们的影响者营销活动时可以做出更好的决策。该公司已与索尼和Safaricom等领先的国际和非洲公司签署了价值超过60万美元的客户合同。 
Digiduka将自己定位为肯尼亚现金经济的数字服务解决方案。 其论点是，非洲的支付解决方案存在两个问题，无法将数百万潜在用户拒之门外。 一种是高昂的交易费用，每笔交易的费用高达9％，另一种是不便的付款方式。 首席执行官和首席技术官之间有超过15年的与非洲领先电信公司合作的经验，并且作为各种创业公司的技术负责人，他们的目标是为肯尼亚的消费者和较小的零售商构建统一的数字服务解决方案。  鹿特丹东非的下一个队列是4月，凯贝德说，通过召集有才华和经验丰富的人才一起在非洲创建杰出的企业，他们希望“鹿特丹”将“帮助培养改变人们思维方式，可持续发展和创新的组织，并鼓励 其他人实现自己的业务目标。”
note_en:
Founded in 2017 by serial entrepreneur, Marcus Grimeland, and a team of experienced entrepreneurs, investors, and company builders worldwide, Antler has raised more than $75 million to help entrepreneurs spread across nine of the world’s major entrepreneurial hubs . They include Amsterdam, Berlin, London, Nairobi, New York, Oslo, Singapore, Stockholm and Sydney.
 Antler’s only office in Africa  is in Nairobi, and it is  run  and led by women.
  Marie Nielsen,  founder of a paper recycling company in Ethiopia called Penda Paper Recycling, is a partner at the firm .  She was an associate partner at Mckinsey & Company responsible for opening their Addis Ababa office .   Melalite Ayenew  is the firm’s tech partner. Her prior experience includes Oracle, Bain & Company, and Princeton Consultants.   Selam Kebede  is the firm’s director and leads operations.  Before joining Antler, she worked for a couple of VCs and entrepreneurship support organizations .
  Similar to  other locations around the world, Antler East Africa runs two cohorts in a year. The firm is particular about adopting a people-first approach, and they  bring together professionals with, on average, 10 years of experience in their respective industries . These professionals who become founders ideate , iterate and create solutions  typically  based on insights they have gathered or problems observed during the course of their past professional experience in their respective industries . After six months of incubation, the firm invests in the teams they can help further.  Typically  in the pre-seed stage, Antler cuts $100,000 checks for a 20% equity in each selected team.
 “Our process is very hands-on; by working with the co-founders over several months, we get the opportunity to help shape the business models and perform extensive due diligence before investing,” Nielsen said to TechCrunch .
 The due diligence Nielsen talks about  is supported by  the global Antler platform, where they pull upon its network of more than 400 experts across technologies and industries .  After the pre-seed investments, Antler East Africa claims to continue to support the teams as they hit the ground running and start raising funds from follow-on investors .
 Ayenew adds that the firm is also exploring the opportunity to invest in pre-existing, early-stage startups developed outside its program, but early enough for them to come in and still provide value  in addition  to the monetary investment .
  Given that Nairobi is Antler’s only office in Africa, the team looks out for founders working on pan-African problems and solutions .  It has attracted founders from more than 15 African countries, which plays a large role in maintaining its cohorts’ outlook to be  organically  pan-African .
 To date, Antler East Africa has invested in a broad range of technology companies in the B2B, B2C and direct-to-consumer space, ranging from emerging sectors like robotics and AI to sectors such as health tech, fintech, and proptech .  From its last two cohorts, Antler East Africa has invested in six startups. They include:
 Cooked , a subscription-based meal kit provider, helps consumers search for, shop, and cook food at home better .  Cooked operates with weekly and monthly subscriptions and delivers products home to its customers on pre-agreed days of the week . The founders have more than 20 years of experience in finance, food, and restaurants industries between themselves.
 UNCOVER  claims to be building the continent’s most trusted skincare brand and content platform by partnering with top skincare labs in Korea .  The company carried out a skincare survey with responses from 1,000 Kenyan women and claims the data obtained will help develop viral knowledge platforms and effective customized products .
 Having spent its early days in FMCG, and particularly with small traders,   ChapChapGo  identified that the lack of simple and affordable tools tailored to the local context was a major challenge for Kenyan businesses to adopt e-commerce .  ChapChapGo enables businesses to transact online in a few minutes with simple invoicing, automatic reconciliation, and faster M-PESA checkouts .
  Anyi Health  wants to improve access to financial support for primary healthcare seekers.  In Nigeria and many other African countries, patients unable to pay their hospital bills  are detained  in the hospital or left untreated .  Anyi Health aims to solve this through a mobile-based point-of-need credit facility, where patients can apply for credit  directly  at the hospital .  The company  just  started its MVP pilot with three hospitals in Lagos, Nigeria and is looking to raise a $300k seed round based on pilot proof of concept .
 AIFluence  is an AI-driven influencer marketing platform.  Founded by advertising veterans, AIfluence enables brands in Africa to make a better decision when launching, managing and evaluating their influencer marketing campaigns .  The company has signed customer contracts worth more than $600,000 with leading international and African companies, including Sony and Safaricom .
 Digiduka  positions itself as the digital service solution for Kenya’s cash economy.  Its thesis is that payment solutions in Africa have two problems shutting out millions of potential users .  One is high transaction fees, ranging as high as 9% per transaction, and the other, inconvenient payment modes .  With the CEO and CTO having between themselves over 15 years of experience working with leading African telcos and as a technical lead for various startups, they aim to build the unified digital services solution of choice for both consumers and smaller retailers in Kenya .
 Antler East Africa’s next cohort is in April, and Kebede says by bringing brilliant and experienced people together to create outstanding businesses in Africa, they hope that Antler “will help foster organizations that change the way people think, are sustainable and innovative as well as encourage other people to realize their own business goals.”

note_en:
Ride hailing taxi app firm Uber must classify its drivers as workers rather than self-employed, the UK's Supreme Court has ruled.
 The decision means tens of thousands of Uber drivers are set to be entitled to minimum wage and holiday pay.
 The ruling could leave Uber facing a hefty compensation bill, and have wider consequences for the gig economy.
 In a long-running legal battle, Uber had appealed to the Supreme Court after losing three earlier rounds.
  Former Uber drivers James Farrar and Yaseen Aslam,  who originally won an employment tribunal against the ride hailing app giant in October 2016, told the BBC they were "thrilled and relieved" by the ruling.
 "I think it's a massive achievement in a way that we were able to stand up against a giant," said Mr Aslam, president of the App Drivers & Couriers Union (ADCU).
 "We didn't give up and we were consistent - no matter what we went through emotionally or physically or financially, we stood our ground."
 Uber appealed against the employment tribunal decision but the Employment Appeal Tribunal upheld the ruling in November 2017.
 The ride hailing taxi app firm then took the case to the High Court, which upheld the ruling again in December 2018.
 The ruling on Friday was Uber's last appeal, as the Supreme Court is Britain's highest court, and it has the final say on legal matters.
  A key point in the Supreme Court's ruling is that Uber has to consider its drivers "workers" from the time they log on to the app, until they log off.
 Uber drivers typically spend time waiting for people to book rides on the app. Previously, the firm had said that if drivers were found to be workers, then it would only count the time during journeys when a passenger is in the car.
 "This is a win-win-win for drivers, passengers and cities. It means Uber now has the correct economic incentives not to oversupply the market with too many vehicles and too many drivers," said James Farrar, ADCU's general secretary.
  However, questions still remain about how the new classification will work, and how it affects gig economy workers who work not only for Uber, but also for other competing apps.
 Mr Aslam, who claims Uber's practices forced him to leave the trade as he couldn't make ends meet, is considering becoming a driver for the app again. But he is upset that it took so long.
 "It took us six years to establish what we should have got in 2015. Someone somewhere, in the government or the regulator, massively let down these workers, many of whom are in a precarious position," he said.
 Mr Farrar points out that with fares down 80% due to the pandemic, many drivers have been struggling financially and feel trapped in Uber's system.
 "We're seeing many of our members earning £30 gross a day right now," he said, explaining that the self-employment grants issued by the government only cover 80% of a driver's profits, which isn't even enough to pay for their costs.
 "If we had these rights today, those drivers could at least earn a minimum wage to live on."
title: 英国最高法院规则优步司机是工人
title_en: UK Supreme Court rules Uber drivers are workers

note:
律师表示，决定意味着驾驶员将有权享有带薪假期等基本权利
    英国最高法院驳回了优步针对具有里程碑意义的就业法庭的上诉，该裁决裁定其司机应被归类为有权获得最低工资和带薪假期的工人。
 六位大法官做出了一项一致决定，支持2016年10月就业法庭的裁决，该裁决可能会影响演出经济中数百万名工人。
  最高法院强调，组织为草拟旨在回避基本保护措施的人为合同而进行的任何尝试都是无效且不可执行的。
  法官批评了优步要求其驾驶员签署的有争议的合同，称这些合同“被视为以阻止驾驶员主张适用法律赋予工人权利为目的”。
 勒加特勋爵在判决书中说，他“不确信”优步与司机的合同安排符合伦敦交通局监督的监管制度。
 App Drivers and Couriers联盟的联合首席索赔人兼秘书长James Farrar表示：“这项裁决将从根本上重新调整零工经济，并通过算法和合同欺骗手段结束对工人的剥削。优步司机被残酷地卖掉了无休止的灵活性和创业自由的梦想。 
“现实是非法的低薪，危险的长时间工作和严格的数字监控。我很高兴由于这项裁定，工人终于可以得到某种补救，但是政府必须紧急加强法律，以便演出工人也可以获得病假工资和免受不公正解雇的保护。”
 TUC秘书长Frances O’Grady说：“没有任何公司可以超越法律。优步必须遵守规则，并停止否认其司机在工作中的基本权利。
 “这项裁定是零工经济工作者和共同体面的重要胜利。虚假的自谋职业剥削了人们，让公司逃避缴纳应得的税款。”
 该案始于两名Uber司机Farrar和Yaseen Aslam代表另外19人将Uber告上法庭，他们辩称他们是由这家位于旧金山的公司雇用的，而不是自己工作。
 这是挑战零工经济工人的自雇身份的一系列案件之一，包括对微型出租车公司Addison Lee以及交付集团CitySprint，Excel和eCourier的诉讼。
note_en:
Decision means drivers will be entitled to basic rights such as paid holidays, say lawyers
    The UK supreme court has dismissed Uber’s appeal against  a landmark employment tribunal ruling that its drivers should be classed as workers with access to the minimum wage and paid holidays.
 Six justices handed down a unanimous decision backing the October 2016 employment tribunal ruling that could affect millions of workers in the gig economy.
  The supreme court highlighted that any attempt by organisations to draft artificial contracts aimed at side-stepping basic protections were void and unenforceable.
  Judges criticised the controversial contracts  Uber asked their drivers to sign, saying they “can be seen to have as their object precluding a driver from claiming rights conferred on workers by the applicable legislation”.
 In the judgment, Lord Leggatt said he was “not convinced” that the contractual arrangements Uber conducted with drivers was compliant with the regulatory regime supervised by Transport for London.
 James Farrar, the co-lead claimant and general secretary of the App Drivers and Couriers union, said: “This ruling will fundamentally re-order the gig economy and bring an end to rife exploitation of workers by means of algorithmic and contract trickery. Uber drivers are cruelly sold a false dream of endless flexibility and entrepreneurial freedom.
 “The reality has been illegally low pay, dangerously long hours and intense digital surveillance. I am delighted that workers at last have some remedy because of this ruling, but the government must urgently strengthen the law so that gig workers may also have access to sick pay and protection from unfair dismissal.”
 Frances O’Grady, the general secretary of the TUC, said: “No company is above the law. Uber must play by the rules and stop denying its drivers basic rights at work.
 “This ruling is an important win for gig economy workers and for common decency. Sham self-employment exploits people and lets companies dodge paying their fair share of tax.”
 The case began when two Uber drivers, Farrar and Yaseen Aslam, took Uber to court on behalf of a group 19 others who argued that they were employed by the San Francisco-based company, rather than working for themselves.
 It is one of a string of cases challenging the self-employed status of gig-economy workers, including action against the minicab firm Addison Lee and the delivery groups  CitySprint,  Excel and  eCourier.
title: Uber司机是工人，英国最高法院裁定
title_en: Uber drivers are workers, UK Supreme Court rules

 欧洲联盟立法者也在积极关注演出工人的条件，因此政策制定者已经面临压力，以澄清演出工作中的法律 - 今天的裁决只增加了这一点。
     最近的几天 - 最可能预期这个判决 - 优步鼓起了欧洲的游说努力，要求放松平台工作。
 优步认为，如果没有从就业法律的雕刻，那么他们的手就是他们能够更好地融洽的人更好的交易。
 它表示推动了一些相同的“原则”，它在第22条投票倡议中推出，骑行着巨头超级和Lyft在加利福尼亚推动了数亿美元的推动，继续赢得努力供货和运输工作从去年就业重新分类。
 然而，在本周的学术研究小组，Fairwork，Fairwork的回应，指责它在减少改变其平台上改善工作条件的能力下降。
 相反，它表示，科技巨头正试图合法化对平台工人的较低水平，比大多数欧洲工人都受益 - 敦促立法者专注于扩大和加强就业保护，而不是将其浇水。
note_en:
The case, which  dates back to 2016, has major ramifications for Uber’s business model in the UK — and likely regionally, as similar challenges are ongoing in European courts.
 European Union lawmakers are also actively eyeing conditions for gig workers, so policymakers were already facing pressure to clarify the law around gig work — today’s ruling only increases that.
     In recent days — and likely in anticipation of this verdict — Uber has kicked off a  lobbying effort in Europe calling for deregulation of platform work.
 Uber argues that without a carve out from employment laws platforms’ hands are tied over how far they can go to offer workers a better deal.
 It says it’s pushing for some of the same ‘principles’ that featured in the Prop 22 ballot initiative which ride-hailing giants Uber and Lyft spend hundreds of millions of dollars pushing in California, going on to  win a carve out for delivery and transport work from employment reclassification there last year.
 However, responding to Uber’s EU white paper  this week, the academic research group, Fairwork, accused it of downplaying its ability to make changes to improve working conditions on its platform.
 Instead, it said the tech giant is trying to  legitimize a lower level of protection for platform workers than most European workers benefit from — urging lawmakers to focus on expanding and strengthening employment protections, not watering them down.
